title: CBH Acquires Narrow-Gauge Locomotives From Wabtec
text: PERTH, Australia--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The CBH Group (CBH) has entered into an agreement with Wabtec Corporation (NYSE: WAB) to acquire 17 narrow-gauge locomotives, marking a significant investment in expanding the co-operative's rail fleet as part of the ‘Path to 2033 Strategy'. Wabtec, a leading global provider of equipment, systems and digital solutions for the freight and transit rail sectors, will build 17 CM20ACi dual-cab, diesel-electric locomotives for CBH. The first tranche of five locomot.

title: Wabtec Announces Second Quarter 2023 Earnings Release Date
text: PITTSBURGH--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Wabtec Corporation (NYSE: WAB) announced it will report 2023 second quarter results before the U.S. financial markets open on July 27, 2023. The company will conduct a conference call to discuss those results with analysts and investors at 8:30 a.m. ET the same day. To listen to the call via webcast, visit Wabtec's website at www.WabtecCorp.com and click on “Events & Presentations” in the “Investor Relations” section. An audio replay of the call will be availabl.

title: Wabtec's Certified Pre-Owned Program Wins Its Largest Order for 69 Locomotives from Six Genesee & Wyoming Railroads
text: PITTSBURGH--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Wabtec Corporation (NYSE: WAB) announced today its largest certified pre-owned locomotive order from six Genesee & Wyoming Inc. (G&W) subsidiaries – Buffalo & Pittsburgh Railroad; Chicago, Ft. Wayne & Eastern Railroad; Connecticut Southern Railroad; Indiana & Ohio Railway; New England Central Railroad and Providence and Worcester Railroad. title: Wabtec (WAB) Expands Further Through $230M L&M Radiator Buyout
text: Wabtec (WAB) acquires L&M Radiator for $230 million to expand its Mining portfolio. Management expects this buyout to boost its EPS (excluding transaction costs) with immediate effect.

title: Wabtec Acquires L&M Radiator, Inc.
text: PITTSBURGH & HIBBING, Minn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Wabtec Corporation (NYSE: WAB) announced today it has acquired L&M Radiator, Inc., a leading manufacturer of heavy-duty equipment radiators and heat exchangers. “We are excited to welcome L&M Radiator to Wabtec,” said Mike Fetsko, President of Wabtec's Freight & Industrial Components business. “This transaction will further Wabtec's presence in premium heat transfer solutions and will extend and complement Wabtec's mining products portfo.
